result:
**Engineering-composition schema guarded patch complete.**

The update adds 4 evidence-backed emphasis points to make workflow assumptions explicit (staged-multi-step patterns, execution handoffs, analytic-pipeline differentiation criteria), documents 3 protected regressions as failure-mode guardrails, and sharpens expected fit patterns based on positive-transfer evidence. All incumbent items preserved. Targets reducing 7 zero-margin analytic-pipeline losses while maintaining 93.3 mean assigned score.

keep: tool wrappers / execution interfaces / reproducible commands
add: emphasize: extract task-specific failure mode before adding procedural scaffolding
emphasize: make staged-multi-step workflow assumption explicit
emphasize: name required execution and verification handoffs
emphasize: state differentiation criteria vs analytic-pipeline on borderline cases
avoid: schema broadening that regresses previously stable task-schema behavior
expected_good_fit: pipeline-pattern tasks with benchmark-threshold or deterministic-output-contract verification
expected_bad_fit: borderline tasks where analytic-pipeline's workflow bias is more central
failure_modes: protected regression on earthquake-phase-association, exoplanet-detection-period, find-topk-similiar-chemicals
failure_modes: low-margin schema-boundary ambiguity against analytic-pipeline
extract the task-specific failure mode before adding broader procedural scaffolding
make the staged-multi-step workflow assumption explicit before rendering task-specific guidance
name the required execution and verification handoffs instead of leaving tool use implicit
state why this schema should win over analytic-pipeline on borderline tasks
schema broadening that regresses previously stable task-schema behavior
pipeline-pattern tasks with benchmark-threshold or deterministic-output-contract verification
tasks requiring staged-multi-step workflows with explicit dependency ordering
borderline tasks where analytic-pipeline's workflow bias is more central to success
protected regression on tasks previously successful under this schema: earthquake-phase-association (100→0), exoplanet-detection-period (60→0), find-topk-similiar-chemicals (31.25→0)
low-margin schema-boundary ambiguity against analytic-pipeline on borderline assignments (7 near-zero-margin losses)

challenger_guidance.conservative: Preserve all incumbent emphasis items. Add only the four new emphasis points that make workflow assumptions and handoffs explicit. Add the single avoid guardrail against schema broadening. Convert protected regressions into failure-mode documentation without changing core behavior.
challenger_guidance.exploratory: Test whether explicitly naming execution/verification handoffs in the emphasize section reduces analytic-pipeline boundary losses. Experiment with making the pipeline-pattern assumption even more prominent in the meta_prompt. Consider whether the protected regressions indicate over-fitting to build/test tasks.
challenger_guidance.differentiating: Sharpen the analytic-pipeline boundary by stating when engineering-composition should win: when the task requires ordered patching with compile/test gates, not just data transformation pipelines. Add expected_good_fit patterns matching positive-transfer evidence (benchmark-threshold verification, dependency ordering). Document protected regressions as explicit exclusions to prevent future mis-assignments.

expected_effect: Reduce analytic-pipeline boundary ambiguity by explicitly stating differentiation criteria for the 7 borderline cases
Preserve positive transfer on adaptive-cruise-control (100), energy-ac-optimal-power-flow (100), and 4 other gains by keeping stable base intact
Guard against future protected regressions by documenting earthquake-phase-association, exoplanet-detection-period, find-topk-similiar-chemicals as failure modes
Improve schema clarity by making implicit workflow assumptions (staged-multi-step, execution handoffs) explicit in emphasize section
Maintain 93.3 mean assigned score while reducing zero-margin losses through sharper boundary definition
acceptance_notes: Verify that the 4 new emphasize additions do not broaden schema scope beyond current stable base
Check that protected regressions (earthquake-phase-association, exoplanet-detection-period, find-topk-similiar-chemicals) are not re-assigned in next round
Monitor analytic-pipeline boundary: expect reduced zero-margin ties on 3d-scan-calc, citation-check, fix-build-* tasks
Confirm that positive-transfer tasks (adaptive-cruise-control, energy-ac-optimal-power-flow) maintain 100% scores
Watch for whether explicit handoff naming reduces tool-use ambiguity on pipeline-pattern tasks
Guarded patch constraint: this update should NOT increase assigned task count beyond stable baseline
